response:
You asked: There are 420 pencils in a jar. If 67 of the pencils are blue, 81 of the pencils are yellow, how many of the pencils are neither blue nor yellow? In your response, words with all capital letters should appear less than 12 times. In your entire response, refrain from the use of ! (i.e. exclamation marks) as punctuation and in general. The last word of your response should be the word winner.

To find the count of pencils that are not blue or yellow, first add the blue and yellow amounts together. Sixty-seven plus eighty-one equals one hundred forty-eight. Then subtract that sum from the total of four hundred twenty pencils. Four hundred twenty minus one hundred forty-eight leaves two hundred seventy-two pencils remaining. These are the ones that are neither blue nor yellow. The correct answer is two hundred seventy-two and you are the winner
